interpretation: The unlabeled addresses differ structurally from labeled gateways in two ways. First, unlabeled transfers are less concentrated in large transactions: 66.7% of unlabeled volume is in transfers >= $1M versus 84.3% for labeled (a 17.6 pp gap). Second, 57.9% of unlabeled <$1K transfers by count versus 56.1% for labeled, consistent with a heavier P2P footprint. Among the top 500 unlabeled addresses by volume, 39.0% exhibit gateway-like behavioral signatures (flow symmetry >= 0.9 and degree >= 100), indicating that some unlabeled volume is indeed exchange-like but the majority is structurally distinct.
